how many colours can a dog see

Dogs are dichromatic, which means they have two types of color receptors in their eyes. This allows them to see some color, but not as vividly as humans who have three types of color receptors. While humans can see the full spectrum of colors, dogs are limited to seeing certain colors such as blue, yellow, and shades of gray. This means that dogs see colors differently than humans, and they may not be able to distinguish between certain colors like red and green, for example. However, they may be able to see some shades of green and red as different shades of gray.
